title: Flaky Moroccan Pastry Tagine
description: A crowd-pleasing Moroccan dish featuring flaky pastry and a sweet, floral flavor profile. This tagine is made with pineapple juice and is kosher-friendly.

ingredients.name: unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: chilled and cut into small pieces

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, confectioners' sugar, and salt.
Add the cold butter to the dry ingredients and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work the butter into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the pineapple juice, orange blossom water, and rose water, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes, until the dough is smooth and elastic.
Roll the dough out into a large rectangle, about 1/4-inch thick.
Brush the dough with the beaten egg and fold the dough in half lengthwise, like a letter.
Cut the dough into 1-inch wide strips and twist each strip into a spiral shape.
Place the pastry spirals on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each spiral.
Bake the pastry for 25-30 minutes, until golden brown.
